response.justification: Bogdan Szabo demonstrates strong full-stack development skills and a diverse technical background, but his experience lacks depth in the core C++ and industrial automation focus required for this role. While he has worked with low-latency systems and event-driven architectures, his recent work is heavily skewed toward web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React) and AI-agent infrastructure, which are not directly aligned with the job's requirements. His open-source contributions and mentorship are positive indicators of technical leadership, but the role demands more specialized expertise in C++, secure engineering, and industrial protocols.
strengths: Extensive experience with event-driven architectures and low-latency systems (e.g., DLang at Dunnhumby, GISCollective platform).
Proven leadership in mentoring teams and fostering technical excellence (e.g., workshops, Refactoring Dojos, open-source contributions).
Strong problem-solving skills and adaptability across multiple domains (web, mobile, desktop, embedded, and IoT-adjacent projects).
concerns: Limited recent hands-on experience with C++ (last notable C++ work was at National Instruments in 2014–2015).
No explicit experience with industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443) or secure development lifecycle (SDL) practices.
Most recent roles focus on web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than the embedded/cloud automation systems central to this role.
